listitem.para: * bd = the name of a database, a string matrix or a list collecting the names (between quotes) of ts or vectors loaded in the environment
* out = the excel file where to save the data
* sep = decimal separator (optional: if not given then sep = '.')
* transpose = anything, but optional: if given then series are exported in rows instead of columns
* sepcol = the separartor used for colums (optional: if not given, then set to the char ' ')
* names = the names to give to the variables in the destination file if the user wants them to differ from their Scilab names (optional)

refentry.refsection: DESCRIPTION Exports the content of a data base or a list of variables bd to a file that Excel can read

refsection.title: EXAMPLE
refsection.programlisting:
expbd2exc(GROCERDIR+'/data/bdhenderic.dat','c:/mydb/mydata.txt') // save on a file 'c:/mydb/mydata.txt' the variables 'rnet','ly', 'lp' and 'lm1' contained in the scilab file GROCERDIR+'data/bdhenderic.dat'
load(GROCERDIR+'/data/bdhenderic.dat'); expbd2exc(list('rnet','ly', 'lp','lm1'), 'c:/mydb/mydata.txt') // same thing as previous example
expbd2exc(GROCERDIR+'/data/bdhenderic.dat','c:/mydb/mydata.txt',',','transp') //same thing as previous example, expect that decimal sperator is ',' instead of '.' and series are exported in columns and not in rows
